reasoning: The Lean statement asserts exactly the two defining conditions of the `Sylow p H` structure applied to `P.subgroupOf H` (i.e., P viewed as a subgroup of H): being a p-group and being maximal among p-subgroups of H. Since `P.subgroupOf H = P.comap H.subtype` is the canonical way to view P as a subgroup of H when `P ≤ H`, this precisely formalizes "P ∈ Syl_p(H)". The hypothesis `hH : P ≤ H` correctly captures "H contains P", and `P : Sylow p G` captures "P ∈ Syl_p(G)". The conjunction of `isPGroup'` and `is_maximal'` is exactly the Sylow condition in Mathlib, so no hypotheses are added or dropped, no quantifier structure is altered, and the conclusion matches the informal claim exactly.
